DTR  |  Listen to a Short Exchange
LSE-MS6-L4-1400P-MSELA6-VMH-LAT-02 • MS ELA Grade 6 • Figurative, Connotative, and Technical Meanings

Breathing Threads

Click   🎧   to listen.
Listen to a conversation between a girl and a boy. Listen carefully. You will hear the conversation only once. After listening, you will answer a question.
Listen to the Conversation
QUESTION:
Why did the person who wrote the label choose the word "breathes"?
Choose the best answer. You may change your answer before submitting.
SMART SUPPORT:
Go back to the conversation. Construct a complete academic justification. State your claim about why the label uses that word, support that claim with reasoning drawn directly from what the boy says, and articulate the conclusion that follows about what the word really means.
CA ELD PI.B.5  |  CCSS RL.6.4 / RI.6.4  |  MS ELA Grade 6: Figurative, Connotative, and Technical Meanings
